authority.normative_source: The RFC Markdown documents in YuemingHub/Ming-Foundation
authority.registry_role: Derived machine-readable index; never overrides source text

requirements.statement: Every material assertion distinguishes subject, speaker, knowledge object type, supporting evidence, confirmation or contestation state, purpose, and permitted period of use.
Implementations represent the minimum knowledge-status vocabulary defined by RFC-0001.
criteria: confirmed fact within a stated scope

requirements.statement: Stored knowledge-status distinctions remain inspectable even when the user interface uses simpler labels.
A high-impact contested assertion should not drive irreversible or punitive action while review is pending, except under a narrowly documented safety duty.
Parent-authored data must not be labeled as the child's confirmed profile.
A model inference must not be hidden or represented as fact.
A disputed label must not be retained without its disputed state being visible.
A temporary crisis state must not become a permanent identity.
Original source and revision history must not be removed from a material assertion.
Contestability must not be denied solely because the speaker is a professional.
Refusal to agree must not be treated as evidence that an assertion is correct.
One family member's private statement must not be exposed to another without a governed basis.
A person reviewing an interpretation should be able to inspect source, speaker, AI involvement, evidence, confidence, active period, dispute state, correction route, and dependent decisions.

Every material collection or use has a traceable basis containing subject, operator, purpose, data scope, access scope, processing or use, retention rule, review point, objection route, and applicable exception basis.
Mandatory or emergency processing must not be misrepresented as freely given consent.

Rights-request support should include access, source explanation, correction, contextualization, restriction, objection, export, deletion, withdrawal, review of automated or high-impact interpretation, and appeal.
Every retention rule states purpose, retained data, storage location, access, review point, deletion or archival trigger, backup handling, and restricted exceptions.
A retention exception is specific, minimally scoped, access-restricted, time- or review-bounded, explained where safe and lawful, and appealable or independently reviewable where appropriate.
All MingOS channels should resolve to a governed identity, permission, memory, and audit layer.
A channel-specific local record must not become an untraceable shadow profile.

General early-access or contact forms should minimize fields, state purpose and retention, identify recipients and request routes, prohibit sensitive case submission without governed intake, avoid pre-checked optional permissions, and separate distinct purposes.
Sensitive child cases must not be collected through a general marketing field without a governed basis.
Private case data must not be reused for model training merely because it exists.
Data must not be scattered so broadly that correction or deletion becomes impossible.
Data must not be retained indefinitely for unspecified improvement.
A retention exception must not be hidden inside general terms.
An emergency or mandatory process must not be described as voluntary consent.
Staff must not receive access broader than their current purpose requires.
Visible deletion must not leave active copies silently retained elsewhere.
Every high-impact safety action has a named responsible role, recorded basis, bounded action, handoff state, and review path.
AI must not become the final unaccountable actor in safety triage or action.
The selected safety response should be the least intrusive action that reasonably addresses the identified risk.
MingOS must not claim to provide emergency response, diagnosis, legal advice, child-protection authority, or guaranteed human response unless actually staffed and governed.
Public and in-product messaging states the system role, AI or human status, response limits, route to local qualified support, and availability limits.
Non-immediate safety classifications should be contestable.
Safety review preserves original evidence, model and policy version, responsible reviewer, classification changes, false-positive or false-negative outcomes, and affected downstream records.
A disputed safety label must not become a permanent identity marker.

requirements.source_section: 8. Special case: operator may be the risk source
requirements.statement: MingOS must not automatically side with an operator who may be causing harm.

requirements.source_section: 8. Special case: operator may be the risk source
requirements.statement: The pathway should support independent review, minimum disclosure, child or third-party protection, conflict handling, and documentation of access restrictions.
Case and cross-family evidence remains purpose-limited, access-restricted, provenance-preserving, and resistant to re-identification.
Public repository records should contain method, limitations, aggregate findings, objections, and decisions.
Public records must not contain raw identifiable family or child material.

Cross-family analysis states purpose, minimizes fields, documents inclusion and exclusion, preserves uncertainty and counterexamples, avoids ranking, restricts raw-case browsing, receives privacy and bias review, and expires or revalidates derived rules.
External model providers must not receive case data merely because the application uses their API.
Raw family cases must not be committed to the public repository.
Recognizable combinations must not be published as anonymous cases.
Consultation data must not be reused for marketing.
Cross-family analysis must not create family scores or rankings.
Rare cases must not be used as model examples without separate review.
Analysts must not access full cases when aggregates suffice.
Generated summaries must not be treated as de-identified by default.
Data must not be preserved indefinitely because future research may be useful.
Every material public claim is traceable to a repository source, evidence source, or explicit capability-status label.
Official public surfaces should use the defined capability-status vocabulary.
Vision must not be worded as a delivered capability.
A public Charter summary states that it is a summary, identifies the governing document, status, version, synchronization date, change history, and discrepancy-report route.
The website must not silently rewrite Candidate or Accepted commitments for marketing.
Material architecture, Charter, product, and governance pages should display source ID, version or commit, status, review date, capability status where relevant, owner, and correction route.
A material claim record should preserve exact wording, audience, supporting source, limitations, reviewer, publication date, and expiry or re-review date.
Public forms state purpose, required and optional fields, recipient, retention, request route, human or automated follow-up, prohibited sensitive content, and relevant privacy notice.
A general early-access form should not solicit identifiable child, crisis, medical, or consultation details.
Official surfaces make visible AI involvement, fallibility, human-review status, available professional role, unavailable emergency or clinical services, and response-availability limits.
A future implementation should provide repository-to-site synchronization, a normative-page manifest, status/version checks, stale-content release controls, discrepancy reporting, and release notes.
